items.title: Overconfident people struggle more to separate real headlines from false ones, study finds
items.summary: Confidence in specific judgments can predict resistance to misinformation, but a general tendency toward overconfidence might predict susceptibility to believing false claims.

items.title: Cultural frameworks may contribute to racial differences in parole decisions
items.summary: Parole candidates may be evaluated more favorably when they discuss their crimes using cultural frames more common among White Americans than among Black, Hispanic and East Asian Americans, according to a study.
